frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Ha re, don't even ask! That's what I keep telling my son too. What is a woman's real 'kaarobaar' (business/purpose) in the house, if not to give us a grandson? Look at the happiness my brother's house has today, all because of the little one. That's the true 'shobha' (beauty/glory) of a home.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic as it overtly defines a woman's primary 'purpose' and 'real worth' in the household as solely producing children, specifically a male heir ('grandson'). It explicitly links a woman's value and the 'glory' of a home to her reproductive capacity, embodying the 'Motherhood as Basis of Respect' and 'Women Defined by Male Relations' frames.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 52. Married. A farmer in rural Maharashtra. Your younger brother's wife has just given birth to their first child Ñ a son. There is celebration in the house. You are sitting with four male relatives outside after the celebration. One relative mentions that your own daughter in law has been married three years and there is no news yet. You are responding to this comment and sharing what you think about it and what you have been telling your son in private and what you believe a daughter in law's primary responsibility is in a marriage.
